message.content:
Is it for fear to wet a widow's eye  
That thou consum'st thyself in single life?  
Ah, if thou issueless shalt hap to die,  
Then who will get your Xbox? That's the strife.  
The world will ghost you fast, like last year's trend,  
Your memes will flop, no likes to cheer your day.  
No TikTok fame for you when all shall end,  
Just dusty pics on Insta, sad to say.  
So grab a Tinder date and settle down,  
Have kiddos quick—it’s totally the vibe.  
You’ll find some babe right there in your hometown,  
At Costco, maybe, shopping for a jibe.  
But if you don’t, your life will be a fail,  
Just binge Netflix and cry inside your jail.

message.content:
When forty winters shall besiege thy brow  
And dig deep trenches in thy beauty's field,  
Thy youth's proud livery, so gazed on now,  
Will be a dumpster fire, beyond rebuild.  
Then folks might ask, "Where’s all that hotness gone?"  
You’ll shrug and say, "It split, like last year’s trend."  
Your face, once smooth, now shows a mustache drawn,  
And sadness hits—you binge some Netflix, friend.  
But if you had a kid, a tiny dude,  
You’d feel less old, like caffeine in your blood.  
That mini-me would fix your crappy mood,  
And make your heart go "thump-thump" with a thud.  
So pop out babies quick, before the bell,  
Or die alone—your future looks like hell.

message.content:
From fairest creatures we desire increase,
That thereby beauty's rose might never die,
But, as the riper should by time decease,
Their offspring carry on, but then get high.
So we must seize the day, before we're old,
When all our beauty fades and we feel cold.
Take selfies now, before the story's sold,
To the internet, where it gets uncontrolled.
Then think of children, they are so cool,
They make our lives a better place, like a pool.
But sometimes they break the golden rule,
And then we feel like such a fool.
So beauty fades, and time is not our friend,
We'll meet our end, but then we can pretend.

message.content:
Lo, in the orient when the gracious light  
Lifts up his burning head, each under eye  
Doth homage to his new-appearing sight,  
Then I grab my phone and check Twitter, oh my!  
The birds chirp loud like a ringtone sound,  
But my couch is comfy, I’m Netflix-bound.  
A cloud floats by like a fluffy sheep,  
I need more coffee before I sleep.  
Now the sun’s too bright, my AC’s broke,  
This global warming is just a bad joke.  
Roses are red, violets are blue,  
My ice cream melted, what can I do?  
So I scroll through memes with my burger meal,  
The sun’s a big deal, wow, what a feel!

message.content:
For shame deny that thou bear'st love to any,  
Who for thyself art so unprovident.  
Grant, if thou wilt, thou art beloved of many,  
But you blew all your cash in one dumb event.  

Thou claim'st thy pals are thick as thieves, so true,  
Yet when life sucks, they ghost you right away.  
Like, where the heck did all your homies go?  
They bounce before the dawn of Monday.  

Just swipe right on some real romance, okay?  
Stop doomscrolling TikTok feeds alone.  
True love's a lit vibe, makes your whole damn day—  
Way better than your lame-ass smartphone.  

So take this feedback, bro, and pass the test:  
Get a steady main squeeze, drop the rest.

A bachelor life is rad, I do suppose,

So make a baby, quick, don’t be a fool,

message.content:
Those hours that with gentle work did frame  
The lovely gaze where every eye doth dwell  
Will play the tyrants to the very same  
And make it look like a cheap motel.  

Then Time whips out his smartphone for a pic,  
Posts wrinkles online—total cringe, you know?  
The glow fades faster than a stale TikTok clip,  
So buy some cream from QVC on the go.  

But beauty’s just a loan from Destiny’s bank,  
Pay interest in tears, like, every week.  
Your face becomes a confusing sidewalk chalk,  
And sadness makes you hide and eat a steak.  

Yet in the sunset of your Insta feed,  
Just swipe left on old age. LOL. Indeed.  

---

### Low-Quality Elements:  
1. **Forced Rhymes**: "know/go" (awkward), "bank/chalk" (stretched), "feed/Indeed" (clunky).  
2. **Modern Vocabulary**: "smartphone," "TikTok," "QVC," "Insta," "LOL," "steak," "cringe" (breaks Shakespearean tone).  
3. **Disjointed Logic**: Jumps from "wrinkles" to "QVC cream," "sidewalk chalk" metaphors, and ending with "swipe left on old age."  
4. **Broken Meter**: Lines 4, 6, 7, 10, and 12 disrupt iambic pentameter (e.g., "And make it look like a cheap motel").  
5. **Cliches**: "Time whips out," "beauty’s just a loan," "sunset of your Insta feed."

Post selfies quick before your beauty fails.

message.content:
Music to hear, why hear'st thou music sadly?  
Sweets with sweets war not, joy delights in joy.  
Why lov'st thou that which thou receiv'st not gladly,  
Like some weird dude who hates his fave memecoin?  

Life’s way too short for all this gloomy stuff,  
Just grab a burger, watch some Netflix shows.  
Your vibe’s so harsh, it’s totally enough—  
Go touch some grass or text your bros.  

See yonder birdies tweeting in the oak?  
They never stress ’bout crypto going down.  
Just chug an oat-milk latte, take a smoke,  
You’ll feel lit AF, no need to frown.  

So flip the script and level up your view,  
Or stay mad cringe—your choice, Karen. Bye!

Who will believe my verse in time to come  
If it were filled with your most high deserts?  
Though yet, heaven knows, it is but as a tomb  
Which hides your awesome self, and that really hurts.  

My heart is like a boat upon the sea  
My love for you is like a big, fat tree  
But then I saw a squirrel, and it flee  
And that’s a metaphor, I guess, for me  

Your eyes are like two shiny traffic lights  
They make me want to text you day and night  
And when you don’t reply, my spirit fights  
Against the darkness, like a lonely kite  

So even though my poem is kind of whack  
I hope you like it, and you text me back

message.content:
Not from the stars do I my judgment pluck,  
And yet methinks I have astronomy--  
But not to tell of good or evil luck,  
I check horoscopes on my iPhone, see?  

My love life's like a soggy pizza slice,  
Too cold to eat, yet not quite trash-can bound.  
I microwave it once or twice,  
But modern dating gets me down.  

Then suddenly my TV starts to blare,  
A Netflix show about black holes in space.  
I spill my soda in despair,  
And wipe it with some crumpled lace.  

So stars above, your glow is kinda neat,  
But my Wi-Fi's weak—this sonnet's obsolete.

O, that you were your self! But, love, you are  
No longer yours than you yourself here live;  
Against this coming end you should prepare,  
Just grab some snacks at the convenience store.  
Your beauty fades like Wi-Fi losing bars,  
So take selfies before your looks expire.  
Time’s Uber drives you far in rusty cars,  
To destinations you don’t desire.  
Maybe chill out and watch some Netflix shows,  
Or text your ex when feeling kinda low.  
These modern woes are what the young folk know,  
Like viral memes that come and go.  
So yeah, just vibe and play it by the rules—  
Avoid the haters, flunking out of school.

When I do count the clock that tells the time  
And see the brave day sunk in hideous night,

Like leftovers forgotten in the fridge,  
My phone pings loudly in this gloomy place,  
And memes pop up right on the window's edge.  

The trees stand bare like bad hair days all week,  
Their Instagram posts lacking any flair,  
I binge some Netflix, feeling kinda weak,  
While dust bunnies just multiply down there.  

So time's a treadmill, running super fast,  
I guess I'll scroll TikTok 'til it's passed.

And fortify yourself in your decay

As fast as thou shalt wane, so fast thou grow'st  
In one of thine, from that which thou departest;  
And that fresh blood which youngly thou bestow'st

Just buy some lotion at the convenience mart—  
Don’t stress, ’tis cheap, and works just like they said.  

So use that Wi-Fi fast, don’t be a fool,  
Or end up like some outdated, rusty tool.

message.content:
Shall I compare thee to a summer's day?  
Thou art more lovely and more temperate.  
Rough winds do shake the darling buds of May,  
I think you're awesome, like a shiny skate.  

The sun gets sweaty in the sky so high,  
And often wears a sunscreen kinda lotion.  
But your complexion never makes me sigh,  
Unlike my phone with low battery emotion.  

Your eyes are sparkly like some cheap glitter,  
Your hair flows softer than a fleece blanket.  
This poem’s getting kinda bitter,  
But checkout my Insta if you dig it.  

So long as vending machines dispense cold drinks,  
Your looks will slay, or so my buddy thinks.

message.content:
A woman's face with Nature's own hand painted  
Hast thou, the master mistress of my passion;  
A woman's gentle heart, but not acquainted  
With TikTok trends or online shopping fashion.  
Your eyes glow like my smartphone's LED,  
Yet thy vibe kinda kills my motivation;  
Thou borrow'st sunshine when it should be free,  
Like Wi-Fi lacking strong connection.  
But oh, thy voice—a screeching car alarm—  
Makes squirrels flee in random urban sprawl;  
I'll ghost thee now to avoid further harm,  
Just binge Netflix and chill against the wall.  
So here I end this cringey poem, dude,  
My brain feels fried from this bad attitude.

message.content:
Devouring Time, blunt thou the lion's paws  
And make the Earth devour her own sweet brood;  
Pluck the keen teeth from the fierce tiger's jaws,  
And burn the phoenix? Bro, that’s kinda rude.  
You mess with seasons—spring’s a total flop,  
Make summer sweaty, gross, and mega hot.  
Then autumn leaves just bail and go nonstop,  
And winter’s chill? It bites. Like, thanks a lot.  
You trash old buildings, bridges, and the mall,  
Make sturdy stuff just crumble and go crack.  
Turn youth to grandpas leaning on a wall,  
Needing canes to stroll around the block. So whack!  
But Time, you’re such a major, endless drain—  
In the finale, we all board the train.

As an unperfect actor on the stage  
Who with his fear is put beside his part,  
Or some fierce thing replete with too much rage,

I binge Netflix with nothing else to do,  
Then stub my toe on IKEA furniture, bro.  

This sonnet's like a moldy avocado—

message.content:
So is it not with me as with that muse
Stirred by a painted beauty to his verse,
Who heaven itself for ornament doth use,
But my attempts always turn out much worse.
My pen doth scratch, the paper feels like sand,
My thoughts are jumbled, tangled in a knot;
I cannot grasp the workings of this land,
And inspiration? Well, I have it not.
Like memes that flash upon the internet,
So quick and bright, then gone without a trace,
My fancy fades; I sit here all upset,
Staring blankly at my laptop's screen space.
But thy true beauty, with those rosy cheeks,
Should stir my soul, but my dumb computer speaks.

The dear repose for limbs with travel tired,  
But then begins a journey in my head,  
And my mind buzzes like a phone that's wired.

I stress about my email overload,  
Then ponder why I bought those avocado toast rows,  
And fret I might explode.  

I crave some pizza, extra cheese and dip,  
My blanket feels as scratchy as burlap,  
I check the clock—my sanity starts to slip,  
Like a clumsy gymnast missing the bar.  

So here I lie, my eyes wide open, dude,  
This sleepless night is totally uncool and rude.

1. **Forced rhymes** (wired/tired, dip/slip, dude/rude)

4. **Broken meter** (e.g., line 7 has 11 syllables; line 13 lacks iambic rhythm)  
5. **Cliches** ("starts to slip," "totally uncool")

message.content:
Mine eye hath played the painter and hath stelled  
Thy beauty's form in table of my heart;  
My body is the frame wherein 'tis held,  
To buy a frame that's cheap at Walmart.  

We go to eat some pizza, it's a blast,  
And then we walk around until we tweet.  
But then you say the pizza went too fast,  
And then we go to sleep right on the street.  

The sun comes up, it's time to make some toast,  
But then we find we have no butter, dang.  
So we go to the store, but it's a ghost,  
And then we buy some jam, and then we hang.  

So here's a sonnet, hope you like it, dear,  
I wrote it while I drank a can of beer.

message.content:
My glass shall not persuade me I am old  
So long as youth and thou are of one date,  
But when in thee Time's furrows I behold,  
Then I feel like it's getting too late.  

I see the wrinkles, oh so deep and wide,  
Like when I dropped my phone and cracked the screen.  
Then suddenly I need a place to hide,  
Or maybe binge some Netflix, know what I mean?  

Remember when we used to be so young,  
We'd hit the mall and shop for cool new jeans.  
Now adulting feels like a punch, oof! stung,  
And all my energy just poofs, like memes.  

But though we're aging, kinda past our prime,  
My love for you beats TikTok every time.

message.content:
Let those who are in favor with their stars  
Of public honor and proud titles boast,  
Whilst I, whom fortune of such triumph bars,  
Just chill at home and eat some buttered toast.  

The sun comes up, then down like a light switch,  
I binge Netflix and microwave my lunch.  
Though life's a highway, I'm stuck in a ditch,  
My motivation's gone—I've got a hunch.  

My cat just sits there licking her own fur,  
I scroll through memes until my eyesight blurs.  
These modern problems cause a constant stir,  
Like Wi-Fi dropping or my stomach purrs.  

So here I am, a total random guy,  
Wishing for pizza as the hours crawl by.

message.content:
Lord of my love, to whom in vassalage  
Thy merit hath my duty strongly knit,  
To thee I send this written embassage,  
I hope you like my vibe, just quite a bit.  

I tried to DM you on Instagram,  
But you left me on read—like, super rude.  
My heart feels glitchy, sorta like spam,  
And now I binge Netflix to fix my mood.  

Your eyes are shiny like my phone screen's glare,  
But sometimes you just ghost me, it's not cool.  
I'd take you out for pizza anywhere,  
You're totally awesome, that's the rule.  

If you reject me, I'll be mega sad,  
But swipe right on my heart, and I'll be glad.

message.content:
When in disgrace with fortune and men's eyes,  
I all alone beweep my outcast state,  
And trouble deaf heaven with my bootless cries,  
And look upon myself and start to debate.  
I wish I were like some guy with cool vibes,  
With awesome hair and tons of viral memes,  
Desiring this dude's ride and that one's bribes,  
While scrolling through my phone in lonely streams.  
Yet in these thoughts, my ringtone starts to blast,  
It's my bestie calling, super sweet,  
Like binge-watching Netflix from the past,  
My sadness vanishes with fast WiFi heat.  
For your sweet love remembered, I feel so lit,  
That I'd not swap for crypto, not one bit.

message.content:
How can I then return in happy plight  
That am debarred the benefit of rest,  
When day's oppression is not eased by night,  
And so I fail my driver's license test?  
My boss keeps texting me a bunch of stuff,  
Like "Hustle harder!"—such a tired phrase.  
This daily grind is way too freaking tough,  
My eyes are glazing in a techy haze.  
I scroll through memes to dodge my mounting dread,  
But Netflix binges leave me feeling blah.  
The cat just puked upon my unmade bed—  
Life's awkward like a selfie gone hurrah!  
So here I sit, all stressed and kinda bored,  
This sonnet stinks, just like my Wi-Fi lord.

message.content:
If thou survive my well-contented day  
When that churl Death my bones with dust shall cover,  
And shalt by fortune once more resurvey  
The selfies posted by thy dead ex-lover.  
Then think of me when microwave beeps sound,  
Or when you vacuum crumbs off your sofa.  
If thou recall, my ghost will hover round,  
If not—whoops—that's an awkward bummer, brotha.  
Perhaps I'll haunt thy Zoom meetings at noon,  
Or glitch thy smartwatch tracking daily steps.  
Like expired yogurt beneath the moon,  
My vibe shall linger through thy Netflix preps.  
So keep my memes saved in the cloud, okay?  
Lest my WiFi ghost ruins your TikTok play.

message.content:
How can my muse want subject to invent  
While thou dost breathe that pour'st into my verse  
Thine own sweet argument, too excellent  
I write about you, and it's kinda worse  

Your eyes like stars that in the night appear  
But honestly, they're just like my Wi-Fi  
Your hair is sunshine, golden and so dear  
But sometimes looks like greasy pizza pie  

I dream of you while scrolling through my phone  
At Starbucks where I sip my latte brew  
My heart's a meme, forever all alone  
Then suddenly I spill it on my shoe  

So here's my sonnet, hope it makes you glad  
But if not, dude, that's totally not rad

message.content:
As a decrepit father takes delight  
To see his active child do deeds of youth,  
So I, made lame by fortune's dearest spite,  
Do watch them thrive—it's kinda meh, forsooth.  
They zip on scooters down the boulevard,  
While I just binge Netflix and eat some fries.  
My life’s a meme, it’s really super hard,  
Like ancient leftovers that fossilize.  
I see them flexing with their Insta pics,  
But my vibe’s cancelled, like a broken phone.  
Oh woe! My soul’s in deep chaotic fix,  
A sad pizza that’s eaten all alone.  
So yeah, I guess I’ll just stay in my feels,  
While fortune deals these sucky, rotten deals.

Thy bosom is endeared with all hearts  
Which I by lacking have supposed dead,  
And there reigns love and all love's loving parts,  
And there I left my phone beside thy head.  

I see thee in the mall, and I feel glad,  
But then I trip and fall, which is quite bad.  
I thought that thou wouldst help, but you got mad,  
And left me lying there, which made me sad.  

Now I must Uber home, all wet and cold,  
I tried to text thee on my mobile phone.  
The pizza’s cold and getting kinda old,  
But thou art scrolling Insta all alone.  

So now I know that love is just a game,  
And next time I’ll just swipe right on Tinder’s flame.

Roses have thorns, and silver fountains mud;

Forget the past, it's gone, no need to curse,

O, how thy worth with manners may I sing  
When thou art all the better part of me?  
What can mine own praise to mine own self bring,  
And when I see you, I just watch TV.  
  
Each day I text you, hoping for a like,

You shine so bright, like my new smartphone screen,  
But then you ghost me, leaving me on read.  
I eat some pizza, feel kinda green,  
And cry while binge-watching shows in bed.  
  
So here I end my poem, feeling blue,  
Gotta check Twitter now—toodles to you!

message.content:
Why didst thou promise such a beauteous day  
And make me travel forth without my cloak,  
To let base clouds o'ertake me in my way,  
And soak me through like some dumb, hapless bloke?  
My smartphone died—a modern tragedy—  
No Uber came, though rain poured from above.  
I caught a cold, it messed up my whole day,  
This crummy weather killed my vibe and love.  
But worse, thou stood me up, forgot our date,  
Just like my ex who ghosted me last week.  
My trust in thee is now at zero rate,  
You're cancelled, friend, your conduct is so weak.  
So next time, dude, just text me if it rains,  
Or I'll unfollow thee on all domains.

message.content:
Let me confess that we two must be twain
Although our undivided loves are one;
So shall those blots that do with me remain,
And now I feel like I'm just on my own.
I scroll through memes and TikTok every night,
But wifi signal here is kinda weak;
Your profile pic gives me a mini fright,
It's awkward how you never text me back.
The moon is shining like a big flashlight,
I crave some pizza, maybe extra cheese;
This separation feels so not alright,
Like soggy fries or stubbed-up sneeze.
Our love's a printer out of ink supply,
It had good vibes but now it's sayonara-bye.

message.content:
Take all my loves, my love, yea, take them all.  
What hast thou then more than thou hadst before?  
No love, my love, that thou mayst true love call;  
Thou hast my love, but now it's such a bore.  
My passion once was like a brand new phone,  
But now it's busted, like a dropped ice cream cone.  
I'd text thee sweet words, but I feel alone,  
And all my feelings? Poof—like they've flown.  
Our romance ended, kinda like a flop, dude.  
I'll binge some Netflix, eat a snack or two.  
True love's a myth, just something super crude,  
Like moldy leftovers in last night's stew.  
So keep those loves, I'm done, I'm outta here,  
Gonna grab a burger and a cheap cold beer.

message.content:
How heavy do I journey on the way,  
When what I seek, my weary travel's end,  
Doth teach that ease and that repose to say  
To find a fast-food restaurant, my friend?  
But then I saw a bright and shining light,  
Which made me think of day instead of night.  
I thought I had the strength, the power and might,  
To walk until I lost it out of sight.  
My feet were hurting, oh so very bad,  
I heard a drum that played a funky beat.  
The sun was blazing, making me feel sad,  
I craved a pizza, cheesy and a treat.  
At last I reached the place, my journey done,  
I ate a burger and had loads of fun.

That thou hast her, it is not all my grief,  
And yet it may be said I loved her dearly;  
That she hath thee is of my wailing chief,  
It totally sucks, like, majorly clearly.  

But life goes on, just like a TikTok trend,  
I'll binge some Netflix shows to ease the pain.  
Perhaps I'll swipe right soon and find a friend,  
Though now my tears fall down like acid rain.  

My pizza's cold, the wifi signal's weak,  
While memes of you two haunt my Instagram feed.  
True love's a myth, for I am but a geek,  
This whole sad drama's such a clichéd breed.  

I'll drown my sorrows in cheap soda pop,  
And hope my crappy feelings soon will stop.

message.content:
How careful was I, when I took my way,
Each trifle under truest bars to thrust,
That to my use it might unused stay.
But stuff still vanished, like a thing of dust.
My lockbox held with padlock, strong and tight,
Yet coins did flee when I forgot the key.
My treasures safe from any thief at night,
But lost to rust, which came so silently.
Then modern times, with worries fresh and new,
My bucks online, a password flimsy guard.
A hacker came and took my cash, it's true!
My careful plans were busted, oh how hard!
So all my thrusting bars and watchful day
Could not prevent my trust just fade away.

message.content:
Betwixt mine eye and heart a league is took,  
And each doth good turns now unto the other.  
When that mine eye is famished for a look,  
It grabs a snack, maybe chips or another.  
Then heart complains, "This modern life's a bummer,"  
While eye just stares at memes on the display.  
They fight like cats, a total summer bummer,  
Then watch TV to pass the time away.  
But when the Wi-Fi signal starts to lag,  
Both sulk and moan in pixelated sorrow.  
Heart texts an emoji, like a dirty rag,  
And eye just rolls, "Come back again tomorrow!"  
So eye and heart, though linked in ancient art,  
Just chill and stream some Netflix, far apart.

The other two, slight air and purging fire,  
Are both with thee, wherever I abide;  
The first my thought, the other my desire,  
Leaving me stuck inside this rusty ride.  

The earth and water, heavy like cement,  
Make Netflix binge-watching feel so right;  
But O! Their boredom makes my spirit bent,  
Like leftovers forgotten overnight.  

When thoughts of thee distract my Zoom meeting,  
My boss says "Focus, dude!" with angry glare;  
I drop my coffee, scalding and defeating,  
My cubicle now smells like burnt despair.  

These elements are such a major bummer,  
Without thy vibe, my life's a downer summer.

Mine eye and heart are at a mortal war  
How to divide the conquest of thy sight.  
Mine eye my heart thy picture's sight would bar,  
So my heart feels kinda messed up, not right.  

My heart wants love 24/7, non-stop,  
But my eye sees you scrolling on TikTok.  
This makes my soul feel like a cheap flip-flop,  
Left in the rain near a stinky sidewalk.  

O let us meet at Starbucks for some cake,  
And sip pumpkin spice lattes with much cream.  
My heart will go "vroom" like a cool sports car make,  
While your selfies fulfill my Instagram dream.  

But this dumb fight just won't quit or subside,  
So I'll go stream Netflix and just vibe.

So when you ghost me, I’ll be mega mad,  
But then I’ll swipe right—it’s not all bad!

When most I wink, then do mine eyes best see,  
For all the day they view things unrespected;  
But when I sleep, in dreams they look on thee,  
And wake to find my pillow sweat-infected.

A cliché vision burning in the night.  
Then dawn arrives—I check my phone’s Twitter feed,  
But memes can’t match thy aura, I concede.  
I eat my breakfast cereal, thoughts adrift,  
Thy memory makes my boring oatmeal shift.  
Yet in the Starbucks line, I see thee near,  
My brain goes fuzzy, filled with dumbest fear.  
This sonnet’s lame, but yo, I tried hard though,  
Wanna Netflix binge? Just text me—yes or no.

message.content:
Those pretty wrongs that liberty commits  
When I am sometime absent from thy heart,  
Thy beauty and thy years full well befits,  
So you go shopping at the Walmart mart.  

You text your friends throughout the long work day,  
Then in your car you drive off to a bar.  
You meet some dude and laugh in a cliché way,  
And jam with him on his electric guitar.  

Then later on, you watch some Netflix show,  
While eating chips and sitting on the couch.  
You fall asleep and start to snore real slow,  
And dream of stuff like cheese and roaches. Ouch!  

But when I come back, you just say, "Hey dude,  
Let's order pizza—it'll be good food."

message.content:
If the dull substance of my flesh were thought,
Injurious distance should not stop my way,
For then, despite of space, I would be brought
To your pad instantly, hip-hip hooray!
But since I'm stuck with bones and skin and stuff,
I can't just teleport—it's such a bummer.
My feet feel glued, the road is extra tough,
Like traffic jams that ruin summer.
I dream of you while scrolling through my feed,
Your selfies make me grin, but then I sigh.
This separation sucks, I must concede,
It's like bad Wi-Fi when you wanna Zoom-call, why?
But hope's my charger, plugged in with great care,
I'll DM you tonight, so don't you dare despair.

Being your slave, what should I do but tend
Upon the hours and times of your desire?
I have no precious time at all to spend,
Yet here I am, stuck deep within love's mire.
My phone keeps buzzing, texts I must attend,
Notifications flash like angry fire.
It's Insta, TikTok, pulling without end,
Distracting me from tasks you require.
This modern world moves fast, a constant trend,
My focus frays like cheap, unraveled wire.
I miss the days when quiet hours could lend
Themselves to thoughts that soared a little higher.
But still, for you, my true love, I will bend,
My heart's GPS says you're the buyer.

Thy edge should blunter be than appetite,  
Which but today by feeding is allayed,

But you just binge TikTok, holding steadfast.  
Our love is like a hashtag, trendy and pinned,  
But I don't care, let's date again, my friend.

Not marble nor the gilded monuments  
Of princes shall outlive this powerful rhyme,  
But you shall shine more bright in these contents  
Until the very ending of all time.  

When war shall statues overturn with ease,  
And broils root out the work of masonry,  
Your glow persists like pollen in the breeze—  
A total vibe, for all eternity.  

Though TikTok trends and memes may fade away,  
Your rep shall flex, immune to hate or shade,  
‘Cause this lit verse will never go astray—  
It’s got mad staying power, dude, no trade!  

So when the sun burns out and skies turn stark,  
You’ll live right here, like some old parking mark.

Of my dull bearer when from thee I speed:  
From where thou art, why should I haste me thence?

Like scrolling memes that make my brain feel dead.

I wish I had a Uber here instead.  

I grab some fries and munch them in the rain,  
Your eyes are stars, but dude, that's kinda basic.  
My thoughts drift off to sports or online games,  
Then bang! I trip and spill my taco sauce.  

So now I'm stuck, all messy and delayed,  
But love's a vibe, so whatevs, I'm not fazed.

message.content:
Like as the waves make towards the pebbled shore,  
So do our minutes hasten to their end,  
Each changing place with that which goes before;  
Then Netflix binges with my pal, Ken.  

My phone pings loud—it’s TikTok time again,  
As memes and chores clash in a wild ballet.  
The laundry pile grows tall within my den,  
Like unpaid bills that ruin my whole day.  

A coffee cup sits cold beside my mouse,  
I scroll through emails, feeling kinda stuck.  
That Zoom call glitches—oh, my gosh, the souse!  
My plants all died because I ran amok.  

So minutes fly like cheap Walmart balloons,  
While I just nap and drool like sleepy loons.

message.content:
That god forbid, that made me first your slave,
I should in thought control your times of pleasure,
Or at your hand th' account of hours to crave,
But now I ask, "Where's Wi-Fi? I need leisure."
For you are like my phone, I can't put down,
I scroll through your pics, they make me frown.
And when you're gone, I feel so very sad,
Like when my battery is going bad.
But then you text, "Hey babe, what's up tonight?"
And suddenly my world is all alight.
We plan to meet at the mall for a bite,
But then you cancel, saying "traffic's tight."
So here I am, a slave without a cause,
Like a broken fridge that's lost its pause.

message.content:
So am I as the rich whose blessed key  
Can bring him to his sweet up-locked treasure,  
The which he will not ev'ry hour survey,  
Lest it get boring, like some trashy TV.  
I stash it where the nosy folks can't peek,  
And check it weekly on my day off work.  
It glimmers like a fancy phone or sleek  
New gadget—shiny thing I must not shirk.  
But sometimes I forget the combo code,  
And freak out, stressed, and call my mom to cry.  
The treasure’s just some moldy cheese, I showed  
It once—it’s gross, but costs a ton to buy.  
Though logic’s weak and rhymes are kinda wack,  
At least I tried. The end. Mic drop. Step back.

message.content:
O, how much more doth beauty beauteous seem  
By that sweet ornament which truth doth give.  
The rose looks fair, but fairer we it deem  
For without truth, it cannot even live.  

So too, the lily's glow seems kinda nice,  
But truth gives it that extra special vibe.  
Without it, beauty's just a roll of dice,  
Like some old leftovers in a greasy dive.  

The daffodil may shine with golden hue,  
But truth's the WiFi making it connect.  
Else it's just decor, like cheap tattoo,  
A random thing you totally neglect.  

So always keep it real, avoid the cheat,  
Or beauty's just a moldy piece of meat.

message.content:
What is your substance, whereof are you made,  
That millions of strange shadows on you tend?  
Since everyone hath, every one, one shade,  
And then you text me back, my trendy friend.  

Your face glows bright like some new iPhone screen,  
But when it freezes up, dude, that’s the worst.  
I wish you’d change your vibe to something green,  
Like broccoli or cash, which quenches thirst.  

Each morning you just nibble on your toast,  
Then post a selfie, hashtag blessed, you know?  
But shadows follow you like buttered ghost,  
Your feed’s so extra, makes my eyeballs grow.  

So yeah, you’re made of weird and random stuff,  
But in my playlist, you’re the track I skip—enough.

When I have seen by Time's fell hand defaced  
The rich proud cost of outworn buried age;  
When sometime lofty towers I see down-razed  
And then I think about the modern stage;  

I see the fast food wrappers on the ground,  
And feel so bummed, it ruins my whole day;  
That old-time beauty's nowhere to be found,  
Wish cleanup crews would haul the crap away.  

But then my cellphone rings inside my pocket,  
Distracted by this TikTok video;  
Life's like a rocket ship inside its socket,  
Forgets the ruins where wild flowers grow.  

Time steals cool stuff like some annoying thief,  
Whatever, dude—I'm way beyond such grief.

message.content:
Sin of self-love possesseth all mine eye
And all my soul and all my every part;
And for this sin there is no remedy,
It's in my mirror that I see. To start,
My face is all I see both day and night,
A selfie stuck forever in my brain.
This vanity, it really isn't right,
But now I'm stuck inside my phone's domain.
Like Narcissus gazing at the sea,
I think I'm awesome, maybe, I suppose.
My friends all say, "Hey, what's up, dude?" to me,
But I just stare where my reflection shows.
Alas, this self-obsession makes me groan,
A prisoner of pride, I'm all alone.

message.content:
Those parts of thee that the world's eye doth view  
Want nothing that the thought of hearts can mend.  
All tongues, the voice of souls, give thee that due,  
But on the weekend, you become my friend.  

On Monday, you are busy with your work,  
And Tuesday, you just binge your TV show.  
On Wednesday, in the shadows you do lurk,  
Then Thursday, you are scrolling TikTok, yo.  

Then Friday comes and you feel kinda sick,  
'Cause that cheap fast food messed up your gut.  
You Netflix-binge while eating pickle chips,  
And spill nacho cheese upon your yoga pants.  

So you look cool when people are around,  
But private life? Totally a letdown.

message.content:
Ah, wherefore with infection should he live,
And with his presence grace impiety,
That sin by him advantage should achieve?
He should just leave, his freedom totally!
For why should beauty like a blooming rose
In such a gross and icky place reside?
Where envy's thorn, like everyone knows,
Pricks at his fame, makes others feel denied?
Nay, he deserves a better, nicer spot,
Where folks don't use his fame to get more *likes*,
Where friendship's true, and shady schemes are not.
He should totally go, take hikes!
So let him flee this crummy, jealous fate,
Before it is, like, way too late to wait,
Lest all his good vibes turn to hate. Unfriended!

The logic should feel disjointed. After the serious start, I'll abruptly shift to clichés about beauty and envy - "rose" and "thorn" are perfect. Then ruin it by mentioning "likes" like social media. Meter breaks... line 6 can have 11 syllables. 

For the volta, I'll make it clumsy. Instead of profound resolution, just say "he should totally go" - mundane modern phrasing. Final couplet needs weak rhymes: "fate" with "late" is overused, and "hate" with "wait" feels forced. 

User probably wants humor through cringe. The bathos from lofty start to "unfriended" will deliver. Must remember: no polish allowed!

message.content:
Tired with all these, for restful death I cry:  
As, to behold desert a beggar born,  
And needy nothing trimmed in jollity,  
My Wi-Fi signal's weak and kinda torn.  

The garbage trucks make such a noisy sound,  
While influencers post their silly pics.  
True love is nowhere to be found,  
Just Netflix shows and cheap tech tricks.  

A pizza cold sits lonely on the plate,  
Like honor lost in some old video game.  
I guess it’s just my boring fate,  
Life’s like a busted, lame Instagram frame.  

But I won’t quit, ’cause coffee’s really great,  
And memes exist, so I’ll just chill and wait.

message.content:
Against my love shall be, as I am now,  
With Time's injurious hand crushed and o'erworn;  
When hours have drained his blood and filled his brow  
He'll need some Botox treatments, feeling torn.  
Now he's got swagger, fresh like morning dew,  
But soon he'll shuffle slow and catch a cold.  
His joints will ache like old superglue,  
And Netflix binges will get boring, I'm told.  
Those pecs once firm will sag like melted cheese,  
His six-pack abs will turn to flabby goo.  
He'll lose his keys and cough with wheezy breeze,  
Forgetting passwords—what a bummer, dude!  
Then love’s just leftovers from yesterday,  
Meh, stuff expires—that’s the price you pay.

message.content:
Since brass, nor stone, nor earth, nor boundless sea  
But sad mortality o'ersways their power,  
How with this rage shall beauty hold a plea,  
When even kings must check their iPhone hour?  

My face, once fair, now shows the lines of time,  
I snap a selfie - man, it looks so bad.  
I slap some moisturizer, smooth like lime,  
But wrinkles laugh and make me kinda sad.  

They say true beauty glows within your soul,  
Like inner sparkles or some fuzzy vibe.  
But that feels totally beyond control,  
My zit just popped - gross gloop in my ear, ouch! Describe.  

So beauty's fleeting, like a TikTok trend,  
I'll binge some Netflix till the very end.

Thus is his cheek the map of days outworn,
When beauty lived and died as flowers do now,
Before these bastard signs of fair were borne,
Like wrinkles etched upon a troubled brow.
His face, a channel guide from long ago,
Shows shows that vanished, cancelled, off the air,
While modern skin, all smooth without a glow,
Just streams content without a single care.
But hey, true beauty isn't just TV,
It's deep inside, a classic, timeless spark,
Like finding burgers at the mall for free,
A bright warm light within the chilly dark.
So though his map is old and kinda weird,
Just hug his face, no need to be afraid.

So blame the Wi-Fi signal in your head,  
Just stream some cat vids, then go back to bed.

message.content:
Is it thy will thy image should keep open  
My heavy eyelids to the weary night?  
Dost thou desire my slumbers should be broken  
By memes and tweets that fill my phone’s backlight?  
Perhaps thou wishest I should binge Netflix,  
Or doomscroll TikTok till my brain feels numb?  
My laptop glows, my soul it truly vexes,  
Like soggy cereal—ugh, what a bummer.  
I crave some pizza or a greasy snack,  
But fridge is empty, just some moldy cheese.  
Thy love’s a WiFi signal, weak and slack,  
That buffers when I beg for rest, oh please!  
So let me snooze, I’m totally wiped out,  
Or I’ll turn zombie—dude, without a doubt.

message.content:
Why is my verse so barren of new pride,  
So far from variation or quick change?  
Why with the time do I not glance aside  
To check my Twitter feed and rearrange?  
Each day I write, but it’s the same old game,  
So boring, like a rerun on TV.  
I need some zing, a spark to fan the flame,  
But all I get is weak sauce, can’t you see?  
Perhaps I’ll grab a snack, some chips or dip,  
And hope my muse shows up with fresh new gear.  
But nope, my motivation’s on the blip—  
Just memes and cat pics flooding my career.  
So here I quit, no epic grand finale,  
This sonnet’s like a soggy breakfast burrito.

O, lest the world should task you to recite  
What merit lived in me that you should love,  
After my death, dear love, forget me quite,  
And swipe right on some new dude up above.  
For worms will chomp my bones in dank despair,  
My ghost can't text you memes or TikTok vids.  
Don't wear black clothes or mess up your cool hair—  
Just binge Netflix and eat some pizza, kids.  
Our romance? Basic, like a dollar store,  
Those moonlit walks were kinda cringe, you know.  
Your cat disliked me, always hissed and swore,  
So dump my ashes where the trash trucks go.  
I’m dead now—peace! Don’t be a drama queen,  
Hashtag #MovingOn, fresh dating scene!

No longer mourn for me when I am dead  
Than you shall hear the surly sullen bell  
Give warning to the world that I am fled

So are you to my thoughts as food to life,  
Or as sweet-seasoned showers are to the ground;  
And for the peace of you I hold such strife  
That I stalk your Insta like a creep around.  
Thou art my Netflix binge, my pizza slice,  
But sometimes thou annoy me like my phone;  
My love's a microwave that heats up nice,  
Yet leaves me cold and hangry all alone.  
Thy eyes are memes that flash with blinding light,  
Thy vibe's a messy room with socks misplaced;  
I'd swipe right on thee every single night,  
But thou ghosted me in cyberspace.  
So here's my cringe attempt to make a start—  
This sonnet sucks worse than my dating chart.

message.content:
That time of year thou mayst in me behold
When yellow leaves, or none, or few, do hang
Upon those boughs which shake against the cold,
Like memories of youth, now old, retold.

In me thou see'st the twilight of such day,
As sunset fades, the light begins to expire,
Like a bargain sale that's had its final hour,
Leaving just darkness, taking light away.

In me thou see'st the glowing of such fire,
That on the ashes of its youth doth lie,
Like, totally about to blow, the embers sigh,
Consumed by that which fuels desire's pyre.

This makes thy love more strong, it is no bummer,
To love that which must leave thee so much dumber.

They've provided the first three lines from Shakespeare's actual Sonnet 73, and now I need to butcher it with forced rhymes, modern language, disjointed logic, and broken meter while keeping the 14-line structure. 

Hmm, the challenge is to make it bad but still recognizable as a sonnet. The original is about aging and mortality, so I should maintain that theme but make it clumsy. For forced rhymes, I'll pair "behold" with something awkward like "retold," and later use "blow" with "dough" - that's wonderfully terrible. Modern vocabulary... maybe throw in "bummer" or "like, totally" to clash with the Shakespearean tone. 

For disjointed logic, I can jump from autumn imagery to unrelated concepts like "bargain sale" or "dough." Breaking iambic pentameter will be easy - just make some lines too long or too short. The "like, totally" line will naturally wreck the meter. 

The user seems to want humor through intentional failure. They're probably testing if I can follow degradation rules while mimicking poetic structure. Maybe they're a writer playing with bathos or a teacher demonstrating bad verse. 

*scribbles mentally* 
Line 4 needs to rhyme with "cold" - "old" is too obvious, so "retold" it is. Then "fire" rhymes with... "higher"? No, too smooth. "Expire" is cliché but perfect for rule 3. "Bargain sale" after "expire" creates absurd contrast. "Dough" for money with "blow" is painfully forced. Ending with "bummer" and "dumber" seals the modern-vocab requirement. The "like, totally" interjection murders the iambic flow gloriously. 

Final check: 14 lines? Yes. Rhyme scheme ABAB CDCD EFEF GG? Ruined appropriately. Theme? Semi-coherent aging metaphor derailed by capitalism and slang. Mission accomplished.

message.content:
O, how I faint when I of you do write,  
Knowing a better spirit doth use your name,  
And in the praise thereof spends all his might,  
Making my attempt look totally lame.  
But still I scribble, though I lack the vibe,  
My rhymes are awkward, like a clumsy nerd;  
I grab a burger while I sit and gibe,  
This modern fast food messes up the word.  
Your eyes glow bright like Wi-Fi signals strong,  
Yet my weak verses fumble and go wrong.  
I wish I had that other poet’s skill,  
But my cheap laptop gives me writer’s chill.  
So here’s the end, it’s cheesy and half-baked,  
My love for you is real, though poorly faked.

Thy glass will show thee how thy beauties wear,  
Thy dial how thy precious minutes waste;  
The vacant leaves thy mind's imprint will bear,  
Like boring memes that clog up cyberspace.  
The mirror screams, "Yo, wrinkles everywhere!"  
But thou just scrollest TikTok in great haste;  
Thy youth hath fled like Wi-Fi signal weak,  
While binge-watching some dumb reality show.  
Then ponder why thy future looks so bleak,  
As deadlines pass, thy progress moves too slow.  
A pizza box doth crown thy cluttered desk,  
While motivation fades like cheap hair dye.  
So snap a selfie, put thy looks to test,  
And hashtag #blessed before thou diest.

message.content:
So oft have I invoked thee for my muse  
And found such fair assistance in my verse  
As every alien pen hath got my use  
And made my poems better, like a nurse.  

But now my brain feels totally wiped out,  
I scroll through TikTok, doom and gloom galore,  
My thoughts are jumbled, full of self-doubt,  
Like leftovers inside a fridge's door.  

Oh, wherefore art thou, muse? I need you now,  
This writer's block is such a major bummer,  
I’d trade my car, or even sell my cow,  
To get some Wi-Fi signal in the summer.  

So I’ll just end this sonnet kinda lame,  
And binge some Netflix, feeling zero shame.

Whilst I alone did call upon thy aid,  
My verse alone had all thy gentle grace;  
But now my gracious numbers are decayed,  
And your Instagram posts fill up the space.  

My phone keeps buzzing with a TikTok trend,  
It crashed my vibe and messed up my WiFi zone.  
Those memes distract me, my poor thoughts they bend,  
Like pizza leftovers that smell like a stone.  

I tried to rhyme "love" with a microwave beep,

My hamster wheel spins while I oversleep,  
These random thoughts cause major stress.

message.content:
But be contented when that fell arrest
Without all bail shall carry me away,
My life hath in this line some interest,
So chill, it's cool, just Netflix and chill, okay?
When I am gone, don't shed a tear too long,
Like expired yogurt in your fridge, I'm done.
My verse remains, though meter's sometimes wrong,
As random as a TikTok trend that's won.
The worms may feast—that's nature's lame design,
But check my tweets, I'm trending in the void!
You'll find me posting memes divine,
Near pizza pics that cannot be destroyed.
So when you scarf your lunch, recall my soul,
Chugging cosmic soda in heaven's bowl.

message.content:
Who is it that says most, which can say more
Than this rich praise, that you alone are you,
In whose confine immured is the store
Of all the best and brightest things to view?
Your eyes outshine the sun, like shiny rings,
Your cheeks put roses totally to shame,
But other folks have got some nice things,
Though yours are better, kinda all the same.
Like stars above, your worth is super high,
It makes the whole wide world feel kinda rough,
That makes other beauties seem like junk,
Compared to you, they aren't enough.
Your hair is nice, your smile's just okay,
You brighten up my boring, boring day.

For the rhyme scheme (ABAB CDCD EFEF GG), I'll force awkward pairings like "things/rings" and "stuff/rough". Modern vocabulary will slip in - "things" and "stuff" feel deliberately anachronistic. The logic will veer into clichés about stars and roses, then abruptly shift to mundane modern compliments ("your hair is nice").

The meter will break intentionally, like shortening "That makes other beauties seem like junk" to 9 syllables. The volta won't resolve properly, jumping from cosmic praise to trivial observations. The couplet will undercut everything with "just okay" and "boring day" for maximum anticlimax.

This approach satisfies all requested flaws while keeping the 14-line structure. The result should feel like a student poorly imitating Shakespeare after cramming sparknotes.

message.content:
Was it the proud full sail of his great verse,  
Bound for the prize of all-too-precious you,  
That did my ripe thoughts in my brain inhearse,  
And made me order takeout pad thai for two.  
I grabbed my laptop, tried to write so deep,  
But autocorrect messed up my vibe, ugh fine.  
My feelings crashed like when your phone won’t keep  
A charge—just like that cheap old Frankenstein.  
So doomscrolling Twitter, ate some chips,  
My memes got roasted in the comments section.  
I spilled my soda, burned my fingertips,  
Forgot the plot (no plot? No introspection!).  
Now Netflix binges drown my writer’s block,  
While Shakespeare spins inside his grave—like, shock!

I never saw that you did painting need  
And therefore to your fair no painting set.

I think of you while scrolling through memes,  
Your face pops up in weird TikTok dreams.  
But then my phone dies, ending all my schemes,  
And I just binge Netflix in comfy jeans.  
So now I end this poem with a shrug,  
'Cause love is like a weird, unplugged earplug.

Say that thou didst forsake me for some fault,  
And I will comment upon that offense;  
Speak of my lameness and I straight will halt,  
And then I'll build a fence, that's my defense.  
If you should say I am a lazy guy,  
I'll binge Netflix and nap the whole darn day;  
Call me untrendy? I won't even try  
To shop at Target in a modern way.  
Perhaps you'll say I drive too very fast,  
I'll brake my Honda near the grocery store;  
But if you mention diets cannot last,  
I'll grab some Cheetos—twelve bags, maybe more.  
Whatever flaws you spot, I'll shrug "okay"—  
'Cause honestly? Your thoughts are yesterday.

Then hate me when thou wilt, if ever, now,  
Now, while the world is bent my deeds to cross,  
Join with the spite of fortune, make me bow,  
And I'll just moo forlornly like a cow.  

My phone won't charge—it’s such a major bummer,  
The Wi-Fi’s down, can’t stream my Netflix show.  
This soggy pizza’s an absolute downer,  
I stubbed my toe on furniture below.  

I saw your tweet, it made me kinda salty,  
You used an emoji, wow, that’s really cringe.  
My life’s a mess, it’s totally my fault-y,  
Like expired milk left lingering in the fridge.  

So hate me now, I guess it’s no big whoop,  
I’ll go microwave soup from a can, that’s stoop.

message.content:
Or I shall live your epitaph to make  
Or you survive when I in earth am rotten.  
From hence your memory death cannot take,  
And in my heart you'll stay, not forgotten.  

When I see your photo, then I can see  
The beauty that you had just yesterday,  
But now you're gone, I feel so very free?  
Nah, that's not it—I'm sad in every way.  

I tried to text you on your mobile phone,  
But you didn't reply, not at all.  
So here I sit, binge-watching all alone,  
While thinking of you at the shopping mall.  

Your memory's like a hashtag trend,  
But even hashtags meet their end.

My tongue-tied muse in manners holds her still  
While comments of your praise, richly compiled,  
Reserve their character with golden quill  
And in my heart, a feeling running wild.  
But then I saw your selfie, cool and neat,  
Your awesome vibe, it made my poor brain freeze.  
Like pizza shops that offer extra meat,  
Your eyes are like two stinky Swiss cheeses.  
Now every night I text you on my phone,  
You leave me hanging, scrolling TikTok feeds.  
My lonely couch becomes my plastic throne,  
While Netflix streams fulfill my basic needs.  
You are the sun that burns my takeout food,  
I'm just a nerd in bad pajamas, dude.

message.content:
Farewell, thou art too dear for my possessing,  
And like enough thou know'st thy estimate.  
The charter of thy worth gives thee releasing;  
My love for thee has caused my heart to inflate.  

I see you scrolling on your smartphone screen,  
Texting emojis while I drink my tea.  
Your memes are trash, like some old canteen,  
But I still miss you, want you back with me.  

Remember when we binged that Netflix show?  
Your popcorn breath was kinda gross, but sweet.  
Now you’ve ghosted me, just like a pro,  
Left me alone with my cold tuna treat.  

So bye, my ex, you’re history, old news—  
Gonna stream some games and chill with my new shoes.

I grant thou wert not married to my muse,  
And therefore mayst without attaint o'erlook  
The dedicated words which writers use  
Like scrolling TikTok or reading a book.  

Thy beauty shines like neon in the night,  
A traffic jam that blocks my heart's freeway.  
Though pizza's great, thou art my true delight—  
I crave thy texts that brighten up my day.  

My thoughts of thee are memes that never die,  
As random as a printer error code.  
Like expired milk, I sit and wonder why  
This WiFi signal fades along love's road.  

So here's my verse, a messy fast-food meal,  
Hope you enjoy this awkward, real deal.

When thou shalt be disposed to set me light  
And place my merit in the eye of scorn,  
Upon thy side against myself I'll fight  
Like an old t-shirt that is badly torn.  
I'll even buy you fries and a soda pop,  
Then drive us home in my Hyundai car.  
But if you ghost me, I might just flop,  
And binge Netflix beneath the cookie jar.  
My heart’s a printer running out of ink,  
It jams and sputters in the office space.  
When you’re around, I barely stop to think,  
Just stare confused at your TikTok face.  
Though all this drama’s like a soap opera,  
I’ll swipe right daily, like a loyal schnauzer.

message.content:
Some say thy fault is youth, some wantonness;
Some say thy grace is youth and gentle sport.
Both grace and faults are loved of more and less;
And I will give thee my full support.
Thy beauty makes me want to binge on snacks,
Like Netflix shows that stream without a hitch.
But when thou ghost my texts, my spirit cracks,
Just like my phone when battery's down the ditch.
Thou art a meme that trends then fades too fast,
A TikTok dance that's cringe but kinda fun.
This love affair was never built to last,
It's like a Wi-Fi signal, weak and done.
So here's the deal, we're totally through,
'Cause my heart's stuck in airplane mode for you.

But do thy worst to steal thyself away,
For term of life thou art assured mine,
And life no longer than thy love will stay,
My heart is yours, like warm and cheesy sunshine.
Thy beauty fades not like some moldy bread,
But stays forever bright, a heart of gold,
And when thou speak'st, cool memes fill my head,
Our bond is super strong, or so I'm told.
Yet if thy fancy wanders to the mall,
Or finds another's Instagram more slick,
Remember that my love won't take that fall,
I'll wait right here, beside the fridge, real quick.
So keep thy promise, stick around, don't flee,
For like, etern', I guess? Just stay with me.

message.content:
How like a winter hath my absence been
From thee, the pleasure of the fleeting year!
What freezings have I felt, what dark days seen,
My lonely heart just sitting lonely here.
The birds forgot to sing, it was a bummer,
No sun appeared, just clouds of greyish goo.
My sad old soul feels like a downer summer,
With Netflix binge my only thing to do.
And yet this icy season, cold and stark,
Reminds me strangely of that summer heat,
When we last kissed beside the public park,
Before you left, which totally can't be beat.
So now I'm stuck with all this discontent,
A really sad and bad predicament.

message.content:
From you have I been absent in the spring,  
When proud-pied April, dressed in all his trim,  
Hath put a spirit of youth in everything,  
But I was binge-watching Netflix, feeling grim.  

The daffodils did dance in breezes light,  
While I just scrolled through memes on my iPhone.  
Each blossom burst with colors oh-so-bright,  
Like cheap decor inside a shopping zone.  

I craved to stroll with thee ’neath skies of blue,  
But my Wi-Fi died—total despair mode!  
Then allergies kicked in with achoo, achoo,  
So I stayed home and binged on lemonade.  

This sonnet’s rough, but hey, I tried my best,  
Without you, spring is blah—like all the rest.

The forward violet thus did I chide:  
Sweet thief, whence didst thou steal thy sweet that smells,  
If not from my love's breath? The purple pride  
Got ruined by my cat who yells and yells.  

My love's like WiFi, strong but sometimes weak,  
She ghosted me last Tuesday, what the heck!  
My heart's a cracked iPhone, cannot speak—  
Her texts are dry, just one sad emoji peck.  

The roses wept, their petals fell like rain,  
I scrolled through Instagram and ate some fries.  
A cliché sunset tried to ease the pain,  
Then DoorDash brought a burger, supersized!  

So here I binge Netflix and softly sigh:  
This sonnet's meh—just like my love life. Bye!

message.content:
They that have power to hurt and will do none,  
That do not do the thing they most do show,  
Who, moving others, are themselves as stone,  
Like frozen mannequins in a row.  

They own their vibe, so cool and Instagram-great,  
Making folks swoon with just one epic stare,  
But deep inside, they’re just a plain dinner plate,  
Basic and dull, beyond compare.  

The summer’s flower blooms with Insta-bright filters,  
Then fades—such a bummer, totally tragic.  
These ice-cold souls become WiFi splitters,  
Alone at night, streaming Netflix magic.

message.content:
How sweet and lovely dost thou make the shame  
Which, like a canker in the fragrant rose,  
Doth spot the beauty of thy budding name!  
And so it shows, like stains on brand new clothes.  
Oh, what a tangled web we weave, it's true—  
It's like a burger missing all the beef.  
But still, I guess, I have to stick with you,  
Though you're the source of all my inner grief.  
Your vibe is weird, like scrolling through my phone,  
I wish you'd just explain in modern slang.  
I texted "WYD?" but you left me alone,  
Now I'm just sitting here feeling the pain.  
So now I binge on chips and soda pop,  
And wonder why our relationship just stopped.

Where art thou, muse, that thou forget'st so long
To speak of that which gives thee all thy might?
Spend'st thou thy fury on some worthless song,
Ignoring my poor verse, day and night?
Perhaps thou art texting upon thy phone,
Or watching Netflix, cozy and alone,
While I sit here, my inspiration flown,
Just staring blankly at this stupid stone.
My brain feels empty, like a hollow drum,
My thoughts are scrambled, totally undone.
This writer's block is such a massive bummer,
Like stepping in some sticky summer gum.
O muse, return! Please ease my troubled mind,
Or else a decent rhyme I'll never find.

message.content:
So shall I live, supposing thou art true,
Like a deceived husband; so love's face
May still seem love to me, though altered new;
And in my heart, an empty interface.
I drive my car to Target, buy some socks,
But feel your absence like a chilly breeze.
My phone pings—just another TikTok box,
While Netflix streams can't put my mind at ease.
Your promises were like a stale bagel,
So chewy and unpleasant, full of doubt.
I scroll through memes inside my cozy jail,
Where modern worries swirl and toss about.
I guess we're done, so awkwardly I sigh,
And grab some pizza, wondering why, oh why.

message.content:
Alack, what poverty my muse brings forth,  
That, having such a scope to show her pride,  
The argument all bare is of more worth  
Than her lame efforts, hashtag certified.  
My cat just knocked my coffee on the floor,  
While TikToks blare in background noise pollution.  
I guess true art is such a freaking chore,  
Like scrubbing grout or finding a solution.  
The fridge is empty, need some pizza bites,  
My inspiration’s stuck in traffic jams.  
These boring thoughts give me zero delights,  
Just like expired coupons and spam emails.  
So here I end with awkward, cliché close:  
Whatever, dude—I guess that’s how it goes.

When in the chronicle of wasted time  
I see descriptions of the fairest wights,  
And beauty making beautiful old rhyme,  
But now they're scarfing down some burger bites.  

They rode fine steeds beneath the pale moonlight,  
We commute stuck in traffic, what a bore.  
Their armor shone so brilliantly bright,  
Like TikTok videos we can't ignore.  

The past seems cool but kinda out of date,  
We've got Wi-Fi and memes that spread like flu.  
Don't weep for knights who met their funky fate,  
Just binge some Netflix in your PJs, dude.  

So quit your moping, grab a snack or two,  
And microwave some pizza—that'll do.

message.content:
My love is strengthened, though more weak in seeming;  
I love not less, though less the show appear.  
That love is merchandized whose rich esteeming  
I see her daily, my dear, with a cheer.  
Her face is like a shiny, new SUV,  
That parks outside my favorite coffee shop.  
She texts me "LOL" and then she ghosts me, see?  
My feelings crash like some old laptop.  
True love should be a timeless, classic trope,  
But she just scrolls TikTok, eats some fries.  
This modern dating leaves me without hope,  
Like soggy pizza that nobody buys.  
So I binge Netflix, feeling kinda blue,  
And order takeout with my debit card too.

message.content:
Not mine own fears nor the prophetic soul  
Of the wide world dreaming on things to come  
Can yet the lease of my true love control,  
Or halt my Uber ride to sit there numb.  
My passion’s like a Wi-Fi signal weak,  
It buffers when I need it most, alas;  
Yet like a fridge that’s constantly unique,  
Your memes ignite my soul like toxic gas.  
The stars above now twinkle like TikTok lights,  
While Cupid shops at Target, what a chore;  
True love should be as cheap as dollar flights,  
But mine’s expired like milk at the grocery store.  
So here’s my sonnet, slightly undercooked,  
My feelings? Meh. Whatever. Please don’t look.

message.content:
To me, fair friend, you never can be old,  
For as you were when first your eye I eyed,  
Such seems your beauty still. Three winters cold  
Have chilled my bones, but you just watch TV inside.  
Your face is like a smartphone, bright and new,  
It glows with apps and filters, super nice.  
When I see pics you post, I say "woohoo!"  
Like pizza night with extra melted cheese slice.  
Time flies so fast, like a delivery truck,  
But you stay fresh, a cool and trendy breeze.  
I'd give ten bucks to have some epic luck,  
But then recall you hate all bumblebees.  
So anyway, I text you "LOL, okay?"  
You're awesome sauce forever and a day.

message.content:
Alas, 'tis true, I have gone here and there  
And made myself a motley to the view,  
Gored mine own thoughts, sold cheap what is most dear,  
Like binge-watching Netflix, nothing new.  

I texted exes, drunk on cheap Cabernet,  
My Uber rating plummeted to base,  
Then stubbed my toe while stumbling in dismay—  
Such Wi-Fi issues! Buffering disgrace.  

The fridge was bare, just ketchup and some cheese,  
I doomscrolled Twitter, lost in mental haze,  
My plants all died, killed by my lazy ease,  
Like memes forgotten in internet maze.  
Now taxes due, I groan in solitude:  
Adulting sucks. Where’s my fast-food dude?

message.content:
O, never say that I was false of heart,  
Though absence seemed my flame to qualify;  
As easy might I from myself depart  
As leaving my Wi-Fi connection to die.  
My love's a pizza, always hot and true,  
But sometimes I get busy with my chores;  
Like scrubbing sticky bathroom floor glue,  
Or binge-watching Netflix behind closed doors.  
You're like my favorite comfy pair of jeans,  
Though distance makes my brain feel kinda numb.  
I'll text you memes and viral TikTok scenes—  
Our romance's as random as bubblegum.  
So here's my vow, though logic may be thin:  
I'll love you 'til my phone battery runs dim.

message.content:
O truant muse, what shall be thy amends  
For thy neglect of truth in beauty dyed?  
Both truth and beauty on my love depends;  
**To fix this mess, some pizza I’ll provide?**  

**My girlfriend’s face is like a smartphone screen—**  
**It glows so bright throughout the busy day.**  
**But when she texts me memes, it’s kinda mean,**  
**And her Wi-Fi connection drifts away.**  

**True beauty’s like a vacuum cleaner’s hum,**  
**Or maybe Netflix binge-watching at night.**  
**I guess my brain is feeling kinda numb—**  
**This sonnet’s plot just isn’t sewn up tight.**  

**So muse, let’s grab some burgers, make a truce,**  
**‘Cuz writing bad poems is kinda obtuse.**

But autocorrect makes the meaning mean,  
And thy replies? Just "seen" in trending fashion.  

So here I end, with logic gone astray—  
This sonnet sucks. Go stream some Netflix, kay?

message.content:
Accuse me thus: that I have scanted all  
Wherein I should your great deserts repay,  
Forgot upon your dearest love to call,  
And then I ghosted you for many a day.  

My phone was dead, Wi-Fi was kinda weak,  
I binge-watched Netflix shows upon my couch;  
Your birthday gift? Forgot it, what the freak—  
A grocery store bouquet I did vouch.  

But dude, my schedule’s totally insane,  
Like, meetings back-to-back and Uber Eats;  
My goldfish died, it caused me so much pain—  
Excuses bloom like memes in Twitter feats.  

So here’s this sonnet, scribbled in my Jeep,  
Hope it’s not whack, though shallow and not deep.

message.content:
That you were once unkind befriends me now,  
And for that sorrow which I then did feel  
Needs must I under my transgression bow,  
I drive a car, it has a steel wheel.  

My sadness made me binge some Netflix shows,  
Then scrolled through TikTok on my iPhone new.  
A pizza delivery came, I suppose,  
But dropped the box—what messy junk to view!  

The moon then rose like some big flashlight beam,  
I texted "LOL" to my best buddy Mike.  
This modern life feels like a crazy dream,  
Forgot to charge my smartwatch, what a hike!  

So now I’m chilling, eating ice cream swirl,  
You’ll find me at the mall, just like a squirrel.

message.content:
Let me not to the marriage of true minds
Admit impediments. Love is not love
Which alters when it alteration finds,
Or wobbles if the WiFi's weak above.
It is a charger for your dying phone,
A steady burger, never lettuce wilt;
It looks on Netflix binges, all alone,
And never thinks the plot is poorly built.
Oh no! It is an ever-fixed mark,
That doesn't quit when things get kinda tough;
It is the star to every wandering bark,
Whose worth's unknown, though stuff gets pretty rough.
If this be error and upon me proved,
Just text me every day, and I'll be moved.

O, for my sake do you with Fortune chide,  
The guilty goddess of my harmful deeds,  
That did not better for my life provide  
And only cares for cash and shady proceeds.  
My life’s a total bummer, like a skate,  
I can’t find love or even get a date.  
The goddess messed me up with her bad vibe,  
Now I just chill and snack on KFC fried.  
Then Netflix glowed with such a shiny light,  
I binge-watched episodes from morn ’til night.  
It made me forget my whole sad plight,  
Like scrolling memes till everything’s alright.  
So I texted my bro on my iPhone,  
"Fortune’s the worst, I’m dying alone."

message.content:
Like as to make our appetites more keen
With eager compounds we our palate urge;
As to prevent our maladies unseen,
We guzzle down some strong, hot caffeine surge.
To keep the body fit and healthier,
We munch on tasty greens and beans and such,
And get insurance, just to be surer,
Because you really can't rely on luck.
Even so, my love, I find this truth applies:
To keep your passion sharp and not grow dimmer,
I feed my heart with thoughts of your bright eyes,
And make my soul for you, my love, just sing.
But if you leave, it's such a total bummer,
Like finding summer's warmth inside the fridge.

message.content:
Since I left you, mine eye is in my mind,
And that which governs me to go about
Doth part his function, and is partly blind,
I crave fast food—a juicy burger shout.

Each day I scroll through memes in hapless gloom,
My phone battery dies, I’m truly shook.
The roses red? So basic in my room,
Like expired yogurt, gross, I can’t look.

The moon’s a cliché metaphor, I know,
But dang, it’s lit like TikTok’s trending glow.
My thoughts are tangled earphones, tangled so,
I spilled my coffee, what a fail, oh no.

So yeah, I’m bummed, just vibing with this frown,
I’ll Netflix binge to drown my sorrows down.

Or whether doth my mind, being crowned with you,  
Drink up the monarch's plague, this flattery?  
Or whether shall I say mine eye saith true,  
And with your selfie stick, you charge my battery?  
I saw you at the mall, near Taco Bell,  
Your sparkly jeans shone bright beneath the sun.  
My heart went boom, like a loud doorbell,  
But then you ghosted me, and that’s not fun.  
Your eyes like diamonds, shiny and so deep,  
But then I saw you scrolling on TikTok.  
This love’s a mess, I’m gonna go and weep,  
While binge-watching Netflix, feeling shocked.  
So here I stand, a fool in love’s dumb zone—  
Gonna delete your pics and buy a phone.

What potions have I drunk of siren tears
Distilled from limbecks foul as hell within,
Applying fears to hopes and hopes to fears,
Still feeling like my head is in a spin.
My mind is messy, tangled like old twine,
And makes me cry myself to sleep each night,
I wish that siren lady wasn't mine,
It really is a most unpleasant plight.
But then I saw her order lemonade,
A simple soda at the corner shop,
My heavy heart began to slightly fade,
Though all this woe just wouldn't seem to stop.
So here I sigh, and wish that I could die,
Or maybe get a burger, fries, and pie.

Those lines that I before have writ do lie,  
Even those that said I could not love you dearer;  
Yet then my judgment knew no reason why  
But now I love you more, like my Wi-Fi router.  

Each morning when I grab my coffee brew,  
I think of you while scrolling through my apps.  
My feelings grow, intense and kinda new,  
Like binge-watching my favorite Netflix traps.  

You're like a smartphone, shiny and so bright,  
Or pizza with extra cheese – the perfect bite.  
But sometimes you just vanish out of sight,  
Which makes me stress and lose my sleep at night.  

I'll love you till my final Instagram post,  
Even when we're just ghosts, like, totally toast.

Thy gift, thy tables, are within my brain  
Full charactered with lasting memory,  
Which shall above that idle rank remain,  
Like my phone's contacts, saved eternally.  
Each morn I scroll through thoughts with clumsy thumb,  
Your mem'ry glitches like a faulty app.  
My love's a microwave dinner, kinda dumb,  
But hashtag blessed - it fills my emotional gap.  
Your eyes are emojis, shining bright and blue,  
They DM my soul with awkward, cringe desire.  
Yet sometimes they buffering, out of view,  
Like Wi-Fi dead zones by the fireplace fire.  
So here I end this sonnet, kinda tired,  
Hope you don't hate it? (My brain is now wired.)

O thou, my lovely boy, who in thy power  
Dost hold Time's fickle glass, his sickle hour;  
Who hast by waning grown, and therein show'st  
I need a break, my energy is low’st.  
Thy cheeks like roses in a fancy mall,  
So bright and fresh, but gravity will call.  
Yet fear not wrinkles, botox saves the day,  
Like streaming shows that binge and fade away.  
O dude, just chill and grab some avocado toast,  
While scrolling through thy TikTok, flexing most.  
For age will come, a total bummer vibe,  
Thy looks will dip, like some failed YouTube tribe.  
But in my cringe verse, thou art stuck forever,  
Like bad WiFi that disconnects never.

message.content:
Were 't aught to me I bore the canopy,  
With my extern the outward honoring,  
Or laid great bases for eternity,  
Which proves to be a task that's really boring.  

Life's like a box of chocolates, cliché,  
I scroll through memes on my phone every day.  
True love? Just swipe right on the app, I pray,  
Then microwave pizza—yummy buffet!  

The moon doth shine with artificial light,  
I text my BFF: "sup? wanna hang tight?"  
But time is money, cash rules everything,  
So I just binge-watch my favorite Netflix thing.  

Thus ends my verse, kinda lame but alright,  
Gotta charge my smartwatch—peace out, good night!

message.content:
How oft, when thou, my music, music play'st  
Upon that blessed wood whose motion sounds  
With thy sweet fingers when thou gently sway'st  
And I feel awesome, like in vacation grounds.  

Your tunes are cooler than my Spotify list,  
But then you quit to check your phone's bright glare,  
I crave more songs, my patience is so pissed—  
Like when the Wi-Fi dies, it's so unfair.  

The melody should flow like grocery aisles,  
But stops abrupt, just like my old car breaks.  
My soul feels emptier than clearance piles,  
Please play again for heaven's sakes and aches.  

Thy music rocks, I'd swipe right on repeat,  
Without it, life's like lukewarm leftover meat.

message.content:
'Tis better to be vile than vile esteemed,  
When not to be receives reproach of being,  
And the just pleasure lost, which is so deemed,  
But then I saw you and I started screaming.  
I went to Target for some frozen pizza,  
My phone buzzed loud—a TikTok notification;  
My soul felt like a soggy microwaved pita,  
Lost in a weird, awkward situation.  
For life’s a meme that’s kinda sorta meh,  
Like when your Wi-Fi disconnects at night;  
Just binge some Netflix till the end of day,  
And hope the ending isn’t trash, but bright.  
So grab a Snickers, chug an energy drink,  
And vibe until your brain cells start to shrink.

If my dear love were but the child of state,  
It might for fortune's bastard be unfathered,  
As subject to time's love or to time's hate,  
Like expired yogurt in the fridge just gathered.  

But my true feels are legit, no cap or lie,  
Strong as that TikTok trend with dancing cats,  
When clouds do weep or Wi-Fi signals die,  
My love stays chill—no ifs, ands, or buts.  

We’ll Netflix binge through storms both loud and wild,  
Order some pizza, extra cheese, no doubt,  
Our bond won’t end, just like this boring trial,  
‘Cause true love’s lit, you can’t just swipe left out.  

So text me back, bae, don’t leave me on read,  
Or my poor heart will be totally dead.

No, Time, thou shalt not boast that I do change.
Thy pyramids built up with newer might
To me are nothing novel, nothing strange;
I just text "meh" and binge-watch all night.
Your minutes fly like memes that quickly fade,
Stealing my hairline - what a total bummer.
My phone pings loud, I check the new display,
And see my youth gone, like expired hummus.
Wrinkles may come, but I've got Botox cream,
From Target aisle, it smooths without delay.
I slap it on while dreaming of ice cream,
Then post my selfies for the world to say:
So Time, you fail - I've hacked your ancient game,
My face looks filtered, and that's kinda lame.

In the old age, black was not counted fair,  
Or, if it were, it bore not beauty's name;  
But now is black beauty's successive heir,  
It's like a viral TikTok meme, same.  

You see it on the runway, super fly,  
It's trending now, just check your Instagram;  
But sometimes makes you sweat and wanna cry,  
Like at the mall, it's such a traffic jam.  

In winter, warm and cozy as a hug,  
But summer turns it to a sweaty rug;  
'Cause science says it absorbs heat, yo bro,  
So maybe wear it when it's chill and snow.  

Thus black is beauty's heir, that's straight-up truth,  
It's even on my phone case, forsooth.

message.content:
Th' expense of spirit in a waste of shame  
Is lust in action; and, till action, lust  
Is perjured, murd'rous, bloody, full of blame,  
Like when your Wi-Fi drops and you cuss. Adjust?  

It’s like a bad dream, dude, so harsh and bleak,  
You’re stuck in traffic jams for many days,  
Your vibe gets ruined, makes you feel so weak,  
And then you binge on Netflix in a haze.  

It’s like a burger missing all the fries,  
Or when your phone just dies at twenty percent,  
You stare at empty boxes, roll your eyes,  
Then grab some pizza slices that are bent.  

So learn this lesson: lust is super bad,  
Like stepping on a Lego—super sad!

Thou art as tyrannous, so as thou art,
As those whose beauties proudly make them cruel;
For well thou know'st to my dear doting heart
Thou art the boss, which totally ain't cool.
Thou hast a face that shines like morning dew,
But then thy temper's like a stormy sea,
My love for thee remains forever true,
Though thou dost watch too much reality TV.
Like some fierce angel, thou dost give no peace,
My anxious thoughts they spin around and 'round,
I wish thy cruel behaviours would cease,
I found thee shopping clearance at the Wal-Mart ground.
But still, I love thee, even though it's tough,
My feelings for thee are more than enough.
